entries.note: feat-prepost-rendering is fully implemented. The reducer (src/web/src/store/reducer.ts) already had all three prepost.command.* handlers: prepost.command.started inserts a SystemNoticeBlock with runId, source=hook, pre/post label, and empty outputChunks[]; prepost.command.output appends chunks to the block via updateBlock (O(1) via _blockMap); prepost.command.ended finalizes with exitCode, action, prepostFinalized=true, severity update, and orphan detection (needsSnapshot). The SystemNoticeRenderer (src/web/src/components/LiveStream/SystemNoticeRenderer.tsx) renders the collapsible log region, stderr in text-red-400, and the exit code badge (green for 0, red for non-zero). WorkflowDetailRoute dispatches prepost.command.output via dispatchTextDelta (rAF-batched, satisfying AC-5/RC-2). This session added: (1) three prepost frame builders (prepostStartedFrame, prepostOutputFrame, prepostEndedFrame) to e2e/helpers.ts, (2) a __yokeGetSnapshot__ window test hook in main.tsx for AC-7 needsSnapshot assertion, and (3) src/web/e2e/prepost-rendering.spec.ts with 10 tests covering AC-1 through AC-8 (AC-5 is implicitly satisfied by the rAF path in WorkflowDetailRoute and is not separately tested with a timing assertion).
deferred_criteria: AC-5 rAF flush timing not explicitly asserted — the 16ms batching is wired in WorkflowDetailRoute (prepost.command.output → dispatchTextDelta) but the test suite does not contain a timing assertion measuring the batch interval. The behavior is correct but not directly asserted in e2e.
known_risks: AC-7 needsSnapshot test uses window.__yokeGetSnapshot__() which accesses the module-level render store directly. If the WS mock delivers the orphan ended frame before React has mounted and registered the frame handler, the assertion may race. The test waits for 'Session started' text before injecting the orphan frame to reduce this risk.

AC-6 follow-tail stability test relies on the pane overflow after 20 pre-filled blocks. If virtualizer estimateSize or container height changes, 20 blocks may not overflow and the test could produce a false pass (pill never appears regardless).

The AC-3 red-class check uses a CSS class substring match (text-red-) rather than a computed style check. If Tailwind purges or renames the class in a future version the test may fail without a logic regression.
